title: BKSY: Top UFO Stock Up 117% in 2026
text: BlackSky Technology, a geospatial intelligence and Earth observation satellite company trading on NYSE under ticker BKSY, has experienced remarkable growth year-to-date. The stock climbed 117% to $40.68 since January 2, 2026, marking a significant turnaround after years of underperformance below the $10 threshold. Over a 12-month period ending May 12, 2026, the shares gained 253% from the previous year's date. Strong Business Fundamentals: The company's first-quarter 2026 results drove much of the enthusiasm. BlackSky reported $20.8 million in quarterly revenue alongside approximately $160 million in secured contracts, including a likely $90 million U.S. Air Force agreement. CEO Brian O'Toole stated the company is off to a strong start to 2026 and emphasized that demand for our Gen-3 capabilities has never been stronger. Upgraded Guidance: Management raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to $130-150 million from the previous $120-145 million range, projecting approximately 30% annual growth. The company's artificial intelligence integration and third-generation satellite deployment showed particular strength, with related services expected to grow 50% throughout 2026. Future Outlook: Technical analysis indicators show BKSY as a Strong Buy, though Wall Street's average price target of $36.61 lags slightly behind current levels, suggesting analysts haven't fully updated projections.
